REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF LILIAN PADUA AN INDIVIDUAL, DAVE MORALES NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE THE TERMS OF PLAINTIFFS ACCEPTANCE OF DEFENDANT VI THANH LE'S OFFERS TO COMPROMISE UNDER CCP SECTION 998 AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HER ATTORNEY IS DENIED. NO AUTHORITY IS CITED FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE REQUESTED RELIEF (FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE THE CORRECT PROVISIONS OF THE SETTLEMENT AND RELEASE AGREEMENT) IS AVAILABLE TO THE PLAINTIFFS. PLAINTIFFS ONLY CITED PROCEDURAL AUTHORITY IS CCP 128(A)(4) WITHOUT ANY CASE AUTHORITY OR ANALYSIS OF APPLICABILITY OF THIS SECTION TO THE 998 OFFERS (E.G. ARE THEY "JUDGMENTS," "ORDERS" OR "PROCESS?"). PLAINTIFF FURTHER FAILS TO POINT TO ANY PART OF THE ACCEPTED 998 OFFER REQUIRING THE DEFENDANT TO AGREE TO A SETTLEMENT AND RELEASE AGREEMENT ON THE TERMS PROPOSED BY THE PLAINTIFF. THE EXECUTED CCP 998 OFFER INCLUDES, INTER ALIA, THE TERMS "PLAINTIFF HEREBY RELEASES ALL CLAIMS" AND "PLAINTIFF AGREES TO SATISFY ALL LIENS ." THOSE ARE THE TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLE F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Jan-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Thomas Purtell, Law offices of Thomas Purtell, 415-722-6291, via zoom for plaintiff
Andrew Klimenko, Gordon Rees, Scully Mansukhani, LLP, via zoom for defendant

Mr. Purtell contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LILIAN PADUA AN INDIVIDUAL, DAVE MORALES NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE THE TERMS OF PLAINTIFFS ACCEPTANCE OF DEFENDANT VI THANH LE'S OFFERS TO COMPROMISE UNDER CCP SECTION 998 AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HER ATTORNEY is DENIED. No authority is cited for the proposition that the requested relief (for the Court to determine the correct provisions of the Settlement and Release Agreement) is available to the Plaintiffs. Plaintiffs only cited procedural authority is CCP 128(a)(4) without any case authority or analysis of applicability of this section to the 998 offers (e.g. are they "judgments," "orders" or "process?"). Plaintiff further fails to point to any part of the accepted 998 offer requiring the Defendant to agree to a Settlement and Release Agreement on the terms proposed by the Plaintiff. The executed CCP 998 offer includes, inter alia, the terms "plaintiff hereby releases all claims" and "Plaintiff agrees to satisfy all liens? ." Those are the terms of the agreement.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charle F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
